steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between Sue and Big Mike. From the notes, the one fact both share is genus membership: both are members of the genus Tyrannosaurus. The correct choice must state that shared fact for both specimens.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The Field Museum of Natural History, where Sue is housed, is located in Chicago, Illinois.’: mentions only Sue and only her location; says nothing about Big Mike or any similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Big Mike is the nickname of a Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen housed at the Museum of the Rockies in Bozeman, Montana.’: mentions only Big Mike; does not compare him to Sue. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The dinosaur fossil specimens Sue and Big Mike are both members of the genus Tyrannosaurus.’: names both specimens and uses “both” to state their shared genus directly, accomplishing the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘While Sue is housed at the Field Museum of Natural History, Big Mike is housed at the Museum of the Rockies.’: contrasts their locations with “While,” emphasizing a difference rather than a similarity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
